content: PlotAttacher (src/plots/attacher.ts, warren-589c / pl-9d6a step 11) is the attach/detach seam for POST /plots/:id/attachments and DELETE /plots/:id/attachments/:ref. Two-method interface (attach/detach) + defaultPlotAttacher production impl + ServerDeps.plotAttacher test seam. Opens a UserPlotClient and routes through PlotHandle.attach/detach; same UserPlotClient-only ACL guarantee as intent/status writes (mx-bd4d67). attach defaults role to 'tracks' when omitted. detach is ref-keyed on the wire but lib-keyed by att-NNN id: the attacher reads the Plot, finds the first attachment with matching ref, and detaches by id; missing ref surfaces PlotAttachmentNotFoundError (404, plot_attachment_not_found). Not fire-and-log (the user is waiting on the result, same posture as intent-editor/status-changer).

Per-kind attachment ref-shape patterns at the warren handler edge (src/server/handlers.ts ATTACHMENT_REF_PATTERNS, warren-589c): seeds_issue /^[a-z0-9_-]+-[a-f0-9]{4}$/, mulch_record /^mx-[a-f0-9]{6}$/, agent_run /^run-[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/. gh_pr / gh_issue / file fall through to the lib's minLength check (refs are free-form for those kinds). Kind whitelist at the handler edge is plot-cli's ATTACHMENT_TYPES enum (seeds_issue|mulch_record|agent_run|gh_pr|gh_issue|file) — the seed body mentioned 'canopy_prompt'/'sd_plan' but those don't exist in the lib's enum; warren follows lib-as-source-of-truth and rejects them with 400 until plot-cli adds them. Both invalidate deps.plotAggregator.invalidate(project.id) on success so a follow-up GET /plots sees the new attachments_count without the 5s TTL.

PlotQuestionAnswerer (src/plots/question-answerer.ts, warren-e1ac / pl-9d6a step 12) is the question-answer seam. assertQuestionAnswerable(plotId, eventId, events) walks the event log to enforce the handler-edge concurrency invariant the @os-eco/plot-cli library does NOT guarantee: (a) a question_posed event with at===eventId exists, (b) no later question_answered event references it (data.question_id === eventId). Throws PlotQuestionNotFoundError (404) and PlotQuestionAlreadyAnsweredError (409) respectively. defaultPlotQuestionAnswerer re-reads events under the open UserPlotClient and re-runs assertQuestionAnswerable to catch races with the resolver-cached handler-edge check. Appends question_answered with data={question_id: eventId, text: answer}. ACL: UserPlotClient hard-codes kind:'user'; AgentPlotHandle.append generic excludes 'question_answered' per HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES, so agent-actor mistake is unreachable at compile time.

title: Defer shape (a) commit-through-reap for .plot/ to warren-343a; shape (b) snapshot+restore landed in pl-d4d6
rationale: Shape (b) (snapshot .plot/ to os.tmpdir before git reset --hard origin in refreshProjectClone, restore after) was the minimum fix to make Plot state durable on the warren host's project clone — unblocks the warren-on-warren dogfood and lets scenario 27 drop its 100ms tail workaround (warren-aa63). Shape (a) — stage .plot/plot-*.{events.jsonl,json} in reap.ts mergePlot() and commit through branch_push so Plot state round-trips to origin/main on PR merge — extends durability across hosts and to operators inspecting GitHub, but has more design surface: warren-authored commits for trivial-merge children (empty_push edge), PR-auto-open interaction for .plot/-only diffs, and concurrent plan-run children producing events.jsonl conflicts (resolved via mergePlotEventsFile's append-only line-union but only after the next refresh fetches both branches). Deferred under warren-343a as its own plan rather than blocking pl-d4d6's quality-gate close. The two shapes compose: once (a) ships, (b) becomes belt-and-suspenders for in-flight host appender writes between dispatch and the next reap.

description: refreshProjectClone's 'git reset --hard origin/<ref>' wipes uncommitted working-tree changes including warren's host-side Plot appender writes to .plot/<id>.events.jsonl and .plot/<id>.json (defaultPlotAppender in src/runs/spawn.ts, defaultPlanRunPlotAppender in src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts, autoTransitionPlotToDone in src/plan-runs/plot-transition.ts never git-commit). Observable: only the last child's run_dispatched survives on disk after a plan-run, plan_run_dispatched and earlier per-child events disappear at the next spawnRun's refresh, and auto-'done' status.json reverts to 'active'. Scenario 27 was masking it with a 100ms events.jsonl tail.
resolution: Shape (b) per pl-d4d6: preserve .plot/ across the reset via defaultPreservePlot wrapper in src/projects/refresh.ts — snapshot regular files under .plot/ (skipping .index.db*) to mkdtemp(warren-plot-snapshot-) before fetch+reset, restore after. Snapshot wins on conflict. Projects without .plot/ short-circuit (byte-identical to pre-fix). Shape (a) commit-through-reap deferred as warren-343a.

description: queryWithRebuildRetry in src/plots/aggregate.ts (warren-7e85) originally rebuilt the index only on a thrown error from client.query(). But .plot/.index.db is gitignored (warren commit 2d7a75f), so a freshly-refreshed project clone has the *.json + *.events.jsonl files but NO index DB. SQLitePlotIndex creates an empty index file on first construction without throwing, so query() returned rows=empty and the rebuild path was never hit — GET /plots came back empty and GET /plots/:id 404'd even though Plots existed on disk. PlotResolver hits the same gated path, so /plots/:id broke too. Discovered dogfooding plot-3e72876d in deployed warren.
resolution: warren-ede7 / v0.4.4: queryWithRebuildRetry now probes .plot/ via fs.readdir when the first query returns empty rows. If at least one non-dot *.json file exists on disk, call client.rebuildIndex() once and re-query before returning. The disk probe distinguishes 'index is stale, rebuild' from 'project legitimately has no plots' so the rebuild cost only lands when there's something to recover. AggregatorPlotClient gained a hasPlotFilesOnDisk(): Promise<boolean> seam — defaultAggregatorClientFactory wraps fs.readdir; tests stub it. New tests at src/plots/aggregate.test.ts pin both the rebuild-when-files-on-disk and no-rebuild-when-empty paths; the existing throw-path retry test is unchanged.

content: PLOT_ID_REGEX (/^plot-[a-z0-9]+$/) in src/plots/id-validator.ts is the canonical Plot-ID shape minted by @os-eco/plot-cli. warren-bae5 / pl-5310 step 2 wires it into POST /runs and POST /plan-runs via assertPlotIdDispatchable() in src/server/handlers.ts: format check is always-on (throws PlotIdInvalidError, 400 code='plot_id_invalid'); existence check fires only when deps.plotResolver is wired (production: src/server/main.ts), throws PlotIdNotFoundError (400 code='plot_id_not_found'). Empty string / undefined pass through unchanged. Layered AFTER ProjectLacksPlotError in createPlanRunHandler so the more-specific project-shape error wins. NewRun.tsx + NewPlanRun.tsx duplicate the regex literal client-side (UI bundle can't import server-side src/plots/index.ts) — keep both copies in lockstep.

defaultPlotFormalizer (src/plots/formalize.ts, warren-d22e / pl-0344 step 8) is the brainstorm-summarize seam for POST /plots/:id/formalize. Non-mutating: reads every agent_message event on interactive runs bound to the Plot via repos.runs.listByPlotId + repos.events.listByRunIds, runs them through extractSuggestedIntent (deterministic marker parser tuned to the brainstorm agent's **goal**: / **non_goals**: -  format), returns {plot_id, suggested_intent, source_message_count}. The Plot is untouched; users accept/edit via POST /plots/:id/intent and transition via POST /plots/:id/status. No LLM call, no async dispatch \u2014 the marker contract lives in src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts's system prompt and the parser anchors it. ServerDeps.plotFormalizer is the test seam; main.ts wires createDefaultPlotFormalizer({repos}).
